LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. DEFENDANT IS A PUBLIC ENTITY THAT CANNOT BE LIABLE FOR COMMON LAW CLAIMS. SEE MIKLOSY VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (2008) 44 CAL. 4TH 876, 899. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING FEHA. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE EXISTENCE OF AN EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY OF MONSON, LAUREN M. ON AUG-21-2009 THROUGH SEP-14-2009 FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-31-2009 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Mark Stewart, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and opposing party; Lauren M. Monson, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3856, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for negligence. Defendant is a public entity that cannot be liable for common law claims. See Miklosy vs. Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 Cal. 4th 876, 899. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action regarding FEHA. Plaintiff must plead facts showing the existence of an employment relationship. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
